stories.headline: US, Iran clash in Hormuz as war escalates: What happened, why it matters
stories.summary: A ceasefire between the US and Iran has been in place since April 8. Both sides are yet to agree on a peace deal.

stories.headline: Oil prices rise after US and Iran exchange fire in Hormuz strait
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says the ceasefire between Washington and Tehran is still in place.

stories.headline: Thousands run Palestine Marathon under shadow of Israel’s war on Gaza
stories.summary: Marathon draws 13,000 runners globally to amplify unity and resilience amid escalating violence in Gaza and West Bank.

stories.headline: Hormuz Transits Halted Since Tuesday as US, Iran Clash
stories.summary: The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to commercial shipping after the US and Iran clashed overnight near the waterway, attacking each other’s assets in the area.

stories.headline: U.S. Strikes Iranian Targets; Iran Says It Returned Fire
stories.summary: The exchange was the latest twist in a week of mixed signals in the region and tensions over the Strait of Hormuz, as President Trump searches for an off-ramp in the war that he started.

stories.headline: South Africa president faces call to resign after court ruling
stories.summary: The Constitutional Court says MPs were was wrong to block impeachment proceedings against Cyril Ramaphosa.

stories.headline: The WW2 spy killed in mystery crash days after the war ended

stories.headline: Aramco, Adnoc Sneak Oil Through Hormuz as Iran Menaces Strait
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia’s Aramco Trading Co. and the UAE’s state oil company Adnoc are among firms that have moved crude cargoes through the Strait of Hormuz since Iran effectively closed the waterway, according to people familiar with the situation.

stories.headline: In Huge Blow to Democrats, Virginia Court Strikes Down House Map
stories.summary: The decision is a major victory for Republicans, wiping away a measure approved by voters to allow Democrats to gain as many as four House seats in the midterms.

stories.headline: Virginia’s high court strikes down voter-passed House map favoring Democrats
stories.summary: The finding is a major setback for Democrats in their effort to counter GOP-led redistricting in other states. The measure approved April 21 gave Democrats an edge in four districts.

stories.headline: Virginia Supreme Court strikes down Democrats’ redistricting plan in US
stories.summary: Virginia's top court blocks redistricting plan, handing Republicans a key win before crucial midterm elections.

stories.headline: Airlines cannot add fuel surcharges after ticket sales, EU says
stories.summary: As fuel prices soar amid the Middle East crisis, Brussels warns airlines they cannot retroactively hike ticket prices — and says costly kerosene alone is no excuse for dodging passenger compensation.

stories.headline: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of breaching Victory Day ceasefire
stories.summary: The two sides report responding to hundreds of drone attacks, just hours into a truce to cover celebrations of the Soviet Union's victory over Nazi Germany.

stories.headline: Three dead after volcano erupts on Indonesian island
stories.summary: Officials had previously issued warnings on climbing the active volcano due to safety concerns.

stories.headline: Waste Industry Is Recession-Resilient: Republic Services
stories.summary: Republic Services CEO Jon Vander Ark joined Bloomberg Open Interest to break down why the waste industry thrives even in downturns. He calls it “recession-resilient” with strong margins. He reveals a major $500M bet on advanced recycling that turns plastic back into new bottles. Plus, why AI will be the true game-changer for the future of waste management. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Gundlach Takes Longshot Bet in Case of US Debt Restructuring
stories.summary: DoubleLine Capital’s Jeffrey Gundlach is repositioning some of his funds for the extreme scenario that the US government could choose to restructure its debt in response to a potential future recession.

stories.headline: Pentagon Releases Files on U.F.O.s
stories.summary: The initial files are murky images that show what could be anything. The government said more would be released on a rolling basis.

stories.headline: Trump Reversed Hormuz Plan After Saudis Denied Airspace Access
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia’s refusal of support suggests that President Trump’s unpredictable approach to Iran has strained ties with one of his closest allies in the Middle East.

stories.headline: ‘Make up their own minds’: Pentagon releases first tranche of UFO files

stories.headline: UK elections – early results and takeaways; will Starmer have to resign?

Leo XIV, Pope's anniversary in Pompeii and Naples: 'We cannot resign ourselves to death'
stories.summary: The Pontiff is on a pastoral visit to Campania, where he relaunched the call for peace that had marked the first words of his papacy. "The disarming and disarming peace" of a year ago in St Peter's Square became a prayer: "May God enlighten those who have responsibility for government".

stories.headline: Third British national has suspected hantavirus infection, UK government says

stories.headline: World Cup fans in China and India face broadcast uncertainty

stories.headline: Markets are banking on the ‘Bliss trade’

stories.headline: Oil’s Price Surge Spurs Nigeria’s Flip From Discount to Darling
stories.summary: Nigerian assets are rallying across stocks, bonds and the currency as investor confidence builds in President Bola Tinubu’s economic agenda.

stories.headline: Stock Strength Driven by Earnings, Not Economy: Santos
stories.summary: The economy is being propped up by AI says JPMorgan’s Gabriela Santos. She joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why the labor market is weaker than headlines suggest, how the AI trade has shifted beyond big tech, and why markets keep rallying despite global chaos. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Enhanced Games Owner Sinks 3.5% After Going Public Via SPAC
stories.summary: Enhanced Group Inc. shares dipped 3.5% after going public via a merger with blank-check company A Paradise Acquisition Corp. that gave it an enterprise value of $1.2 billion.

stories.headline: Itau Asset’s Serra Sees Bulk of Brazil Rate Cuts After Elections
stories.summary: The bulk of interest-rate cuts in Brazil is likely to come only after October elections, according to former Central Bank director Bruno Serra.

stories.headline: Treasuries Gain After Mixed Jobs Data Cements Bets on Steady Fed

stories.headline: Rubio Meets Meloni as U.S.-Italy Relations Strained and Trump’s Attacks on Pope

stories.summary: Signs of labor-market strength drove stocks toward record highs, bolstering speculation that the world’s largest economy remains resilient in the face of an energy shock triggered by the Iran war.

stories.headline: Trump pledged to free Jimmy Lai. Now U.S. lawmakers want action at China summit.

stories.summary: The announcement follows recent ousting of several top officials in China's military.

stories.headline: 'I wouldn't pay it' - Trump on USA ticket price
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says he "wouldn't pay it either" when asked about the potential ticket price to watch his country play Paraguay in their 2026 World Cup opener.

stories.headline: Compass Breaks Brazil IPO Drought With $650 Million Offer

stories.summary: In an opinion piece in The Washington Post, the Pennsylvania senator wrote that while he was “at odds” with the party’s approach to some issues, his values had not changed.

stories.headline: Solomon Islands to get new leader after PM ousted in no-confidence vote
stories.summary: Jeremiah Manele toppled after months of political upheaval in the nation seen as one of China’s closest partners in the Pacific Solomon Islands prime minister Jeremiah Manele lost power in a no-confidence vote in the South Pacific country’s parliament, ending months of political uncertainty.Parliament was adjourned to allow the governor general to make arrangements for the election of a new prime minister. stories.summary: Ukraine's president says Russia has "spurned the ceasefire" and Kyiv is deciding on further action to take.
